neighbors.name: James Allen Massey & Debbie E. Massey
neighbors.claims: Polk County property records list James Allen Massey (also recorded as James A. Massey) and his wife Debbie E. Massey as owners of multiple land parcels in the county [Polk County Tax Assessor](https://polkga.org/departments/tax_assessor/index.php). There is no record of James or Debbie Massey voicing any opinions on development in Polk County; their names do not appear in local news archives or county meeting minutes in recent years [AP News](https://apnews.com/search?query=%22James%20Massey%22%20Polk%20County%20GA).

approach_recommendations.engage: Approach via a personable, local contact to build rapport and listen to any concerns about property impacts, given their lack of a stated stance.

neighbors.name: Kaysie Taylor & Heather Dawn Holder
neighbors.claims: Kaysie Taylor and Heather Dawn Holder are listed together as co-owners of a Polk County property, per county tax records [Polk County Tax Assessor](https://polkga.org/departments/tax_assessor/index.php). Kaysie Taylor works as an X-ray technologist at a nearby hospital in Paulding County, indicating a professional background outside of agriculture [Bold.pro](https://us.bold.pro/my/kaysie-taylor). There are no public reports or meeting records indicating that either Taylor or Holder has taken a public position on local development issues [Coosa Valley News](https://coosavalleynews.com).

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage with an open, informative approach to answer questions and gauge any concerns, as neither co-owner has shown prior involvement in community development debates.

overview_summary: Both profiled neighbors have not publicly taken a stance on local development, with their positions remaining unknown. None hold formal community leadership roles, and high-influence individuals are absent among these neighbors, indicating a generally low influence risk profile.
